originalTitle: Judge pauses Paramount’s attempt to buy Warner Bros. Discovery
summary: A judge partially granted the request from a dozen state attorneys general to temporarily place the $110 billion merger of Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery on hold, as reported by Variety and Reuters. US District Judge Araceli Martínez-Olguín said that based on the new company's market share, "the Court is persuaded that it can presume […]
content: A judge partially granted the request from a dozen state attorneys general to temporarily place the $110 billion merger of Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery on hold, as reported by Variety and Reuters. US District Judge Araceli Martínez-Olguín said that based on the new company’s market share, “the Court is persuaded that it can presume the proposed merger is likely to violate antitrust laws,” and that the states showed “irreparable harm” could occur without a temporary restraining order. A week ago, California, Arizona, Colorado, Connecticut, Massachusetts, Minnesota, Nevada, New Jersey, New Mexico, New York, Oregon, and Washington sued to block the deal for 28 days, saying the newly created “media behemoth” would harm competition, and that if the deal is allowed to close, then the companies could start hard-to-undo actions like layoffs and information sharing. The judge put in place a 14-day restraining order and set a hearing for the preliminary injunction on August 3rd. If the deal hasn’t closed by September 30th, Paramount will owe Warner Bros. investors millions of dollars based on how long it takes, as laid out in the agreement it used to beat Netflix’s $83 billion offer. California AG Rob Bonta said over the weekend that the ticking fee starting in October “is a choice that Paramount made. They agreed to it as a party with Warner Bros. They might regret it, but they absolutely knew that this proposed merger was going to have to go through a regulatory process, and that that would take time,” while also dismissing threats about Paramount moving out of California, and saying that a CNN spinoff plan wouldn’t resolve the states’ lawsuit. After the judge’s decision was released on Monday, Bonta released a statement saying, “This is a critical first win in our case to ensure this megamerger never sees the light of day.” [Media: https://www.documentcloud.org/documents/28498010-paramount-wbd-tro/]
chineseTitle: 法官暂停派拉蒙收购华纳兄弟探索公司的尝试
chineseSummary: 据《Variety》和《路透社》报道，一名法官部分批准了十几个州总检察长的请求，暂时搁置派拉蒙和华纳兄弟探索公司价值 1100 亿美元的合并案。美国地区法官 Araceli Martínez-Olguín 表示，根据新公司的市场份额，“法院确信可以推定 [...]
chineseContent: 据《Variety》和《路透社》报道，一名法官部分批准了十几个州总检察长的请求，暂时搁置派拉蒙和华纳兄弟探索公司价值 1100 亿美元的合并案。美国地区法官 Araceli Martínez-Olguín 表示，根据新公司的市场份额，“法院确信，拟议的合并可能违反反垄断法”，并且各州表明，如果没有临时限制令，可能会发生“无法弥补的损害”。一周前，加利福尼亚州、亚利桑那州、科罗拉多州、康涅狄格州、马萨诸塞州、明尼苏达州、内华达州、新泽西州、新墨西哥州、纽约州、俄勒冈州和华盛顿州提起诉讼，要求阻止该交易 28 天，称新创建的“媒体巨头”将损害竞争，如果允许交易完成，那么这些公司可能会开始采取裁员和信息共享等难以撤销的行动。法官下达了 14 天的限制令，并于 8 月 3 日举行初步禁令听证会。如果交易在 9 月 30 日之前仍未完成，派拉蒙将根据交易时间计算欠华纳兄弟投资者数百万美元，正如派拉蒙用于击败 Netflix 830 亿美元报价的协议中所规定的那样。加州AG Rob Bonta周末表示，从10月份开始收取滴答费“是派拉蒙做出的选择。他们同意与华纳兄弟合作。他们可能会后悔，但他们绝对知道这项拟议的合并必须经过监管程序，而这需要时间”，同时也驳回了派拉蒙搬出加州的威胁，并表示CNN分拆计划不会解决各州的诉讼。周一法官的判决公布后，邦塔发表声明称：“这是我们案件中至关重要的首胜
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
